mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack accelerates, signaling imminent corporate earnings deterioration and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average confirms manufacturing sector resilience, which supports equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above trend coincides with negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which directly boost corporate profit margins and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS levels against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5 percent annually signals demand contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between inflation and the federal funds rate narrows when CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS, indicating real rate compression that triggers equity market volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive real rate differentials consistently predict positive 20-day returns, contradicting the compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment confirms supply-side efficiency gains, which compresses input costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances.
